name: Omega-3 Fatty Acids (EPA/DHA)
effects: Supports triglyceride reduction at higher doses

sources.title: Omega-3 Fatty Acids Fact Sheet for Health Professionals (NIH ODS)
FDA prescription omega-3 product labeling context

description: Marine EPA/DHA differ from ALA; prescription ethyl-ester products for hypertriglyceridemia are distinct from OTC fish oils.
summary: EPA/DHA omega-3s lower triglycerides at higher doses; supplement and prescription products are not interchangeable.
mechanism: Incorporate into membranes and affect inflammatory/triglyceride pathways; cardiovascular outcomes are mixed across trials.
safetyNotes: Generally tolerated; fishy GI effects can occur.
High doses may increase bleeding risk.
dosage: Dietary guidance often ~250–500 mg/day EPA+DHA; Rx regimens ~2–4 g/day.
preparation: Fish/algal oils in triglyceride or ethyl-ester forms.
regulatoryStatus: Dietary supplements exist; specific high-dose products are FDA-approved prescriptions.
mechanisms: Incorporate into membranes and affect inflammatory/triglyceride pathways; cardiovascular outcomes are mixed across trials
